title: What is the definition of a 'wetland' according to Sri La...

messages.content: What is the definition of a 'wetland' according to Sri Lankan policy?

According to Sri Lankan policy, a ‘wetland’ is defined as an area of land where the soil is saturated with water either permanently or seasonally, or where the soil remains inundated for a period or periods during the year. Wetlands include marshes, swamps, bogs, floodplains, rivers, lakes, and estuaries. [National Wetland Policy, p. 3-5]
